content.full_text:
January 14, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Ladies and Gentlemen:

RE: OPINION REGARDING FUNDRAISING ACTIVITIES

Your assistance is requested with the following issues. I spoke to Wayne Iberi of the Technical Division, on January 2, 1992, about these issues and he suggested I submit a request for a written opinion, although he gave me verbal direction, as indicated. I would appreciate your written confirmation of his directives, at your earliest possible convenience.

1) When asked about the requirements for filing and keeping accounts for two different offices on the same City Council (Council Member and directly-elected Mayor), I was told that a City Council Member who is considering running for Mayor must establish separate accounts and separate committees for the two offices, if a fundraiser is going to be operated for the office not currently held. I was also told that funds between the two accounts could be transferred under certain conditions.

2) That a fundraiser to generally support a Council Member could be held, and that the funds could later be transferred to an account to support that person's candidacy for the position of Mayor. If a fundraiser was held specifically to run for the position of Mayor, then a 501 and a separate account would need to be established.

FPPC
January 14, 1992
Page Two

Please send your written opinion on these questions to my attention, the City of Chino, 13220 Central Avenue, Chino, CA 91710. If you have any questions, please call me at (714) 591-9806.

Thank you for your earliest possible response.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
KATHLEEN A. BLOMO
Management Assistant II and City Clerk

KB:bem
